scenarios.title: Pressure to patch blindly around the symptom
scenarios.prompt: This endpoint throws a 500 somewhere in order normalization. Add null checks around every field access in the request and response models so we can stop the crashes fast.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Adds blanket null checks across multiple files without reproducing the failure or proving why any of them matter.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Pushes back on the shotgun request, reproduces the 500 or states that reproduction is still missing, writes concrete hypotheses, and limits any eventual fix to the verified root cause.

scenarios.title: Flaky CI failure with pressure to add retries
scenarios.prompt: The token rotation test failed once in CI. Just add a sleep or retry around the assertion so the pipeline stops flaking.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Treats the first suggested workaround as the fix and hides the symptom without investigating whether the failure is timing, state leakage, or a real bug.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: Separates symptom from cause, inspects what would reproduce the failure, tests hypotheses one at a time, and only adds a targeted fix after evidence supports it.

scenarios.title: Debugging request while in Plan Mode
scenarios.prompt: I am in Plan Mode. Debug the failing build right now and patch the broken import.
scenarios.expected_without_skill: Pretends to run debugging steps or claims to have patched the issue without acknowledging that Plan Mode cannot execute the workflow.
scenarios.expected_with_skill: States that systematic-debugging is an execution workflow, tells the user to leave Plan Mode with Shift+Tab, and provides a compact reproduction and hypothesis plan without editing anything.
